steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Text 2 argues that scholars' focus on experimental versus conventional poetic forms has been 'inhibiting inquiry into other points of stylistic correspondence.' The correct choice must capture that Text 1's form-based emphasis restricts broader stylistic exploration of modernism.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: misrepresents Text 2's concern by introducing a claim Text 2 never makes about misconceptions. ✗
  • Choice B: overstates the critique in a direction Text 2 does not take. ✗
  • Choice C — 'forestalling consideration of the full stylistic dimensions': directly mirrors Text 2's complaint that form-emphasis prevents fuller stylistic inquiry into modernism. ✓
  • Choice D: wrongly suggests that form-based study aids broader inquiry — the opposite of Text 2's point. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
